message.content:
Adversarially VERIFY this research claim about how a top deep-research system scores on DeepResearch Bench.

CLAIM: CORRECTION, HIGH PRIORITY: Tavily is NOT SOTA and ADORE (0.5265) is NOT the SOTA target. On the official DeepResearch Bench leaderboard, tavily-research is 52.44 and ranks 23rd of 45. The real leader is 58.03 (Gemini-2.5-Pro judge) / 55.78 (GPT-5.5 judge). 'ADORE' does not appear on either leaderboard at all.
DETAIL: I pulled the live leaderboard CSVs directly from the benchmark's HuggingFace space (muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard, lastModified 2026-06-28), which is the maintained successor to the stale deepresearch-bench.github.io page (that page still shows the 48.88 Gemini number you got burned by). Two CSVs exist because the benchmark switched judges on 11 May 2026. GEMINI-2.5-PRO JUDGE (data/leaderboard.csv, 45 systems): qianfan_deepresearch_0430 58.03 | ZTE-Nebula 57.27 | Link 57.08 | zhipu 57.06 | xiaoyi 57.00 | ... | cellcog-max 56.67 | ... | tavily-research 52.44 (comp 52.84, insight 53.59, IF 51.92, read 49.21) | ... | gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch 49.71 | openai-deepresearch 46.45 | langchain-open-deep-research 43.44 | perplexity 40.46 | grok 38.22. GPT-5.5 JUDGE (data_gpt55/leaderboard.csv, only 10 systems re-scored so far -- and THIS is the judge POLARIS is pinned to): cellcog-max 55.78 (comp 56.34, insight 57.08, IF 55.30, read 51.94) | WhaleCloud-DocChain 54.78 | bodhi 54.07 | lunon 53.51 | dalpha 53.10 | sourcery 51.17 | gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch 49.98 | openai-deepresearch 47.84 | perplexity 43.05 | grok 41.22. Tavily has NOT been re-scored under GPT-5.5. Implication: POLARIS at 0.4382 under the GPT-5.5 judge is comparable ONLY to that second list; the true gap to SOTA is ~0.12 (to cellcog-max 0.5578), not 0.088. Tavily's blog headline 'SOTA on DeepResearch Bench' is not supported by the leaderboard -- it was likely true only against a narrow comparison set (they benchmark themselves against LangChain's Open Deep Research, 43.44). Do NOT plan against Tavily as the ceiling. Local copies: /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/leaderboard_gemini25pro_judge.csv and leaderboard_gpt55_judge.csv.
SYSTEM: other
SOURCE GIVEN: https://huggingface.co/spaces/muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard/resolve/main/data/leaderboard.csv
STATED CONFIDENCE: high

Go to the source. Check it actually says this. Check it is CURRENT (the benchmark's leaderboard and evaluator both
changed in 2026 — the original paper's numbers are stale; Gemini-2.5-Pro was replaced by GPT-5.5 as the RACE judge).
Default to UNSUPPORTED if you cannot confirm it from a primary source. We would rather have five verified facts
than thirty plausible ones.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

---

colorTo: indigo
sdk: docker
app_file: app.py
pinned: false
license: apache-2.0

# DeepResearch Bench Leaderboard

A comprehensive benchmark and leaderboard for evaluating Deep Research Agents.

## Overview
This Hugging Face Space hosts the interactive leaderboard for the DeepResearch Bench project.

Visit our [project website](https://deepresearch-bench.github.io) for more information.

### Local Development
```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
python app.py
```

## Hugging Face Space Details
- SDK: Docker

content.text:
All numbers verified byte-for-byte against the live primary source.

**What I did:** Fetched both CSVs directly from the HuggingFace space at the given URL (plus the `data_gpt55/` sibling), pulled the space's API metadata, and inspected `app.py` / `tabs/leaderboard_tab_gpt55.py`.

**Every checkable assertion holds:**

- `data/leaderboard.csv` — exactly 45 systems. Header order top→down: qianfan_deepresearch_0430 **58.03**, ZTE-Nebula-DeepResearch-V20260519 57.27, Link 57.08, zhipu_deep_research 57.06, xiaoyi 57.00, WhaleCloud-DocChain 56.81, cellcog-max 56.67. `tavily-research,52.44,52.84,53.59,51.92,49.21` sits on data row **23 of 45** — comprehensiveness/insight/IF/readability all match the claim digit for digit. Tail also matches: gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch 49.71, openai-deepresearch 46.45, langchain-open-deep-research 43.44, perplexity-Research 40.46, grok-deeper-search 38.22.
- `data_gpt55/leaderboard.csv` — exactly 10 systems, led by `cellcog-max,55.78,56.34,57.08,55.30,51.94`, then WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612 54.78, bodhi 54.07, lunon 53.51, dalpha 53.10, sourcery 51.17, gemini 49.98, openai 47.84, perplexity 43.05, grok 41.22. **Tavily is absent** — confirmed not re-scored.
- **"ADORE" does not appear** in either CSV (case-insensitive grep, zero hits).
- Space `lastModified` = `2026-06-28T15:39:16.000Z`, matching the claim.
- The judge switch is documented *inside the space itself*: `tabs/leaderboard_tab_gpt55.py` renders `> **Eval Judge**: GPT-5.5 (race) + GPT-5.4-mini (fact-check)` and `Re-evaluation with GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.4-mini is pending.` This independently corroborates that the GPT-5.5 board is the current evaluator and that the 35 missing systems (incl. Tavily) simply haven't been re-run.
- The space README links to `deepresearch-bench.github.io` as "our project website", so this HF space is the official leaderboard host — the "maintained successor" framing is sound.
- Local copies at `/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/leaderboard_gemini25pro_judge.csv` and `/home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/leaderboard_gpt55_judge.csv` are **md5-identical** to what I just fetched live. No tampering, no staleness.
- Gap arithmetic checks out: 0.5578 − 0.4382 = 0.1196 ≈ 0.12.

**Two minor unverified sub-points (neither load-bearing):** the specific "11 May 2026" switch date does not appear anywhere I could find in the space, and POLARIS's own 0.4382 / judge pinning is an internal fact outside this source's scope. The claim about Tavily's blog headline being "true only against a narrow comparison set" is a reasonable inference, not something the leaderboard states.

The core correction is right, and the strategic implication is right: **Tavily at 52.44 is 23rd of 45 and is not a meaningful ceiling.** Under the GPT-5.5 judge POLARIS is actually scored by, the only valid comparison set is the 10-system board, where the target is cellcog-max at 55.78.

input.reasoning:
Verified directly against the primary source (the live HuggingFace space muset-ai/DeepResearch-Bench-Leaderboard), not a secondary summary. Every checkable number in the claim matches byte-for-byte.

GEMINI-2.5-PRO JUDGE (data/leaderboard.csv): exactly 45 systems. Leader is qianfan_deepresearch_0430 at 58.03, followed by ZTE-Nebula 57.27, Link 57.08, zhipu 57.06, xiaoyi 57.00, WhaleCloud-DocChain 56.81, cellcog-max 56.67. The row `tavily-research,52.44,52.84,53.59,51.92,49.21` sits at data row 23 of 45 — so "52.44, 23rd of 45" is exactly right, and the sub-scores (comp 52.84, insight 53.59, IF 51.92, read 49.21) match digit for digit. Tail matches too: gemini-2.5-pro-deepresearch 49.71, openai-deepresearch 46.45, langchain-open-deep-research 43.44, perplexity-Research 40.46, grok-deeper-search 38.22.

GPT-5.5 JUDGE (data_gpt55/leaderboard.csv): exactly 10 systems, led by cellcog-max 55.78 (comp 56.34, insight 57.08, IF 55.30, read 51.94), then WhaleCloud-DocChain_0612 54.78, bodhi 54.07, lunon 53.51, dalpha 53.10, sourcery 51.17, gemini 49.98, openai 47.84, perplexity 43.05, grok 41.22. Tavily is absent — confirming it has NOT been re-scored under the current judge.

"ADORE" appears nowhere in either CSV (case-insensitive grep, zero hits), so 0.5265 is not a leaderboard figure at all.

Corroborating evidence beyond the CSVs: (1) space lastModified = 2026-06-28T15:39:16.000Z, matching the claim; (2) the space's own UI code (tabs/leaderboard_tab_gpt55.py) renders the text "**Eval Judge**: GPT-5.5 (race) + GPT-5.4-mini (fact-check)" and "Re-evaluation with GPT-5.5 / GPT-5.4-mini is pending", which independently confirms the judge switch and explains why only 10 of 45 systems appear on the new board; (3) the space README links to deepresearch-bench.github.io as "our project website", establishing this HF space as the official/maintained leaderboard host rather than a third-party mirror; (4) the local copies at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/leaderboard_gemini25pro_judge.csv and leaderboard_gpt55_judge.csv are md5-identical to what I fetched live, so they are neither stale nor altered.

Arithmetic also checks: 0.5578 - 0.4382 = 0.1196 ≈ 0.12, so the restated gap-to-SOTA is correct.

Minor caveats, none load-bearing: the specific "11 May 2026" judge-switch date is not stated anywhere I could find in the source (the switch itself is confirmed, just not that date); POLARIS's own 0.4382 and its judge pinning are internal facts outside this source's scope; and the assertion that Tavily's "SOTA" blog headline was "likely true only against a narrow comparison set" is a reasonable inference rather than something the leaderboard asserts. The load-bearing corrections — Tavily is not SOTA (23rd of 45), ADORE is not on the leaderboard, and the real GPT-5.5-judge target is cellcog-max at 55.78 — are all fully confirmed.
input.corrected: No correction needed — the claim is accurate. (For completeness, the only unverifiable details are the "11 May 2026" switch date, which does not appear in the source though the switch itself is confirmed by the space's own UI text "Eval Judge: GPT-5.5 (race) + GPT-5.4-mini (fact-check) ... Re-evaluation is pending", and the interpretive remark about Tavily's blog comparison set.)
